perturbation.original_text: Airspan will provide Distributor with such commercial and technical assistance and training as may reasonably be necessary, in Airspan's sole judgment, to enable Distributor to effectively carry out its activities under this Agreement and, in connection therewith, to provide such sales promotional materials, as Airspan may deem appropriate. Airspan will provide such technical support as is reasonable and necessary to maintain the product line as a viable competitor in the market.
perturbation.changed_text: Airspan will provide Distributor with commercial assistance, at Airspan's sole discretion, to enable Distributor to carry out its activities under this Agreement. No technical assistance or training will be provided.
perturbation.explanation: This change omits the provision for technical assistance and training, directly contradicting Section 3.3, which states that the Distributor will train and maintain a sufficient number of technical and sales personnel in order to serve the demands and needs of its customers for Airspan Products, service, and support and carry out the obligations of Distributor under this Agreement. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the distributor is obligated to do something that Airspan will not help them to perform.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor will train and maintain a sufficient number of technical and sales personnel in order to: (a) serve the demands and needs of its customers for Airspan Products, service, and support; and (b) carry out the obligations of Distributor under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'No technical assistance or training will be provided' by Airspan. However, the contradicted text mandates that the Distributor 'train and maintain a sufficient number of technical and sales personnel'. This creates a direct contradiction because the Distributor is obligated to provide training and maintenance, but Airspan, according to the changed text, will not provide the necessary assistance, which was originally intended in the original text. This creates a situation where the distributor is obliged to carry out activities while the supplier will no longer provide assistance for that activity. This creates a strong in-text contradiction. Therefore, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: The Contract Price for each item of Equipment, Installation Services, or Software is as specified in Schedule IV and shall be paid to Airspan in US Dollars (US$) free of any withholding tax and of any currency controls or other restrictions. The Contract Price includes: a. The price of the Equipment; b. The fee for the licensing of the Software; c. If Installation Services are ordered and such order is accepted by Airspan, the charges for installing and testing each unit of Equipment or Software; d. The charges for the warranty of the Equipment and Software in accordance with Sections 14 and 15; and e. Costs in accordance with delivery obligations set forth in Sections 8.1 and 8.2. [*]
perturbation.changed_text: The Contract Price for each item of Equipment, Installation Services, or Software shall be paid to Airspan in US Dollars (US$) free of any withholding tax and of any currency controls or other restrictions. The Contract Price includes: a. The price of the Equipment; b. The fee for the licensing of the Software; c. If Installation Services are ordered and such order is accepted by Airspan, the charges for installing and testing each unit of Equipment or Software; d. The charges for the warranty of the Equipment and Software in accordance with Sections 14 and 15; and e. Costs in accordance with delivery obligations set forth in Sections 8.1 and 8.2.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Schedule IV, where the contract prices are specified, the contract creates an in-text contradiction and becomes unenforceable. Because Schedule IV isn't included now, the entire payment structure is ambigious. It contradicts all payment-related clauses and sections referring to it, especially Section 7.1, which mentions using the Contract Price when issuing invoices, since the Contract Price is as specified in Schedule IV.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Airspan will issue an invoice (the "Invoice") to Distributor in accordance with the payment terms below. The Invoice specifically will identify the Equipment, Software, or other items shipped, and the Contract Price of such items. Any Installation Services or other services provided by Airspan will be invoiced separately following completion of such services. The Invoice also shall state the total due to Airspan from Distributor (the "Invoice Total"), which shall include the Contract Price, and any applicable taxes, duties, and other fees due pursuant to Section 6 of this Agreement. Payment of the Invoice Total is due (without any right of set-off) Net forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the Contract Price was located in Schedule IV. The changed text removes this reference, making the determination of the Contract Price ambiguous. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states 'The Invoice specifically will identify the Equipment, Software, or other items shipped, and the Contract Price of such items' because without Schedule IV, the Contract Price cannot be determined, rendering the invoicing process unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees to purchase and stock a comprehensive inventory of spare parts based upon the recommended list set forth in Schedule III to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees to purchase and stock spare parts as deemed necessary by Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the obligation to follow the 'recommended list' in Schedule III and replaces it with 'as deemed necessary by Distributor.' This directly contradicts Section 3.8, which speaks about maintenance performed in accordance with Airspan's latest maintenance standards, and the removed part means that there are no more Airspan's standards, since the inventory isn't purchased following Airspan's list. This create an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor agrees to train and have readily available a maintenance staff to efficiently and expeditiously maintain or upgrade all Airspan Products purchased or licensed by Distributor from Airspan. Maintenance shall be performed in accordance with Airspan's latest maintenance standards for Airspan Products, as provided by Airspan, by Distributor's own personnel and may not be subcontracted or delegated to any other person or entity without Airspan's prior, written authorization.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated adherence to Schedule III for spare parts inventory. The changed text gives the distributor sole discretion, negating the Airspan's maintenance standards referenced in the contradicted text, which requires maintenance to be performed 'in accordance with Airspan's latest maintenance standards'. The change creates a direct conflict because the distributor is no longer obligated to use Airspan's recommended parts, potentially leading to non-compliant maintenance.
